lines.text: "Order will become a matter of chemistry." Ulre Corbin is already pumping neuro-chemical stabilizers through the air systems to manage people's anxiety.
What's fascinating is how Nyland frames Corbin's psychology.
His parents' divorce, escaping shouting in the kitchen with the draft chilling his ankles? This man is carrying so much loss, and I think that's why he's so obsessed with control.
I think he doesn't see a distinction between managing himself and managing the colony. It's all just variables to optimize. Emotions to suppress.
Let's shift to Centurion Jax because that training scene was brutal. The deck is designed to match Mairee's treacherous nature."

Jax clearly has daddy issues, honestly. The way he treats those recruits is like how his father treated him. That's pretty sad. And when Kirsten Strand shows up, we get that devastating observation that she'd be leading the Guard "if she'd been born with the right genitalia." Nyland isn't being subtle about power structures here.

Strand immediately reads the whole situation too. Jax is shipping her, the rookies, and the retiring veteran to the Delta to let the jungle kill his problems for him. She sees everything. I already love her.

The Delta itself is fascinating as a setting we haven't fully seen yet. It's described as this place that "wanted to rot everything they built," with spore storms and humidity that destroys infrastructure. It's clearly going to be important.

The next scene shifts to Eva Bellamy up in orbit, playing this incredible chess game. She controls the Corridor, she has the military advantage with her Vanguard ships, but she's choosing to support Corbin. Not out of loyalty to him—

Right, loyalty to his system. To the numbers. Her father taught her that "space didn't reward good intentions; only a perfect grasp of the numbers did." She's a pragmatist who's decided that Corbin's data-driven approach is the only viable path. She's seen the numbers. The terminal diagnosis.

But she's also quietly working the captains. That private call with Laris where she essentially says "your plan is good, I'll help you, just do it through proper channels"—she's managing rebellion by co-opting it.
Nyland has set the board. These are the opening moves.
But chess follows a grid. I don't think that's the game Mairee is playing...
